2605.18630.title: SCICONVBENCH: Benchmarking LLMs on Multi-Turn Clarification for Task Formulation in Computational Science
2605.18630.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as scientific AI as- sistants, and a growing body of benchmarks evaluates their capabilities across knowledge retrieval, reasoning, code generation, and tool use. These evaluations, however, typically assume the scientific problem is already well-posed, whereas practical scientific assistance often begins with an ill-posed user request that must be refined through dialogue before any computation, analysis, or experiment can be carried out reliably. We introduce SCICONVBENCH, a benchmark for multi- turn clarification in scientific task formulation across four computational science problem domains: fluid mechanics, solid mechanics, materials science, and par- tial differential equations (PDEs). SCICONVBENCH targets two complementary capabilities: eliciting missing information (disambiguation) and detecting and correcting erroneous requests containing internally contradictory information (in- consistency resolution). Our benchmark pairs a structured task ontology with a rubric-based evaluation framework, enabling systematic measurement of LLM per- formance across three dimensions: clarification behavior, conversational grounding, and final-specification fidelity. Current frontier models perform relatively well on inconsistency resolution, but even the best model resolves only 52.7% of the disambiguation cases in fluid mechanics. We further find that frontier LLMs fre- quently make silent assumptions and perform implicit specification repairs that are not grounded in the conversation with users. SCICONVBENCH establishes a foundation for evaluating the upstream conversational reasoning that a reliable computational science assistant requires. The code and data can be found at https://github.com/csml-rpi/SciConvBench.
2605.18630.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作科學AI助手，並且越來越多的基準評估它們在知識檢索、推理、代碼生成和工具使用方面的能力。 然而，這些評估通常假設科學問題已經被良好地提出，而實際的科學協助往往始於一個不明確的用戶請求，這必須通過對話來精煉，然後才能可靠地進行計算、分析或實驗。 我們介紹了SCICONVBENCH，這是一個針對科學任務表述中多輪澄清的基準，涵蓋四個計算科學問題領域：流體力學、固體力學、材料科學和偏微分方程（PDEs）。 SCICONVBENCH針對兩種互補的能力：引出缺失信息（消歧義）和檢測及修正包含內部矛盾信息的錯誤請求（不一致性解決）。 我們的基準將結構化任務本體與基於評分標準的評估框架相結合，實現了對LLM性能在三個維度上的系統測量：澄清行為、對話基礎和最終規範的忠實度。 當前的前沿模型在不一致性解決方面表現相對良好，但即使是最好的模型在流體力學中也僅解決了52.7%的消歧義案例。 我們進一步發現，前沿LLMs經常做出默默的假設，並進行隱式的規範修正，這些修正並未在與用戶的對話中得到支持。 SCICONVBENCH為評估可靠的計算科學助手所需的上游對話推理建立了一個基礎。 代碼和數據可以在https://github.com/csml-rpi/SciConvBench找到。
2605.18630.author: Nithin Somasekharan et.al.
2605.18630.authors: Nithin Somasekharan, Youssef Hassan, Shiyao Lin, Gihan Panapitiya, Patrick Emami, Anurag Acharya, Sameera Horawalavithana, Shaowu Pan
